title: Audit Index Growth vs Licensed Retention (Capacity Risk)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 2.9 (Audit storage capacity) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.23: Audit Index Growth vs Licensed Retention (Capacity Risk).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.23' running on /services/data/indexes for ot_audit, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0 title=ot_audit
| eval gb=currentDBSizeMB/1024, days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,1)
| eval daily_gb=gb/30
| eval days_to_full=if(daily_gb>0, round((maxTotalDataSizeMB/1024)/daily_gb,1), null())
| table title, gb, days, daily_gb, days_to_full
description: Forecasts `ot_audit` disk usage to avoid silent loss of security audit records before policy retention is met.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Set `maxTotalDataSizeMB` with headroom; (2) route verbose protocols to warm/cold tier; (3) expand frozen path; (4) alert if `days_to_full`<90.
visualization: Single value, Gauge, Table. Key fields: `daily_gb`, `days`, `days_to_full`, `gb`, `title`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for forecasts `ot_audit` disk usage to avoid silent loss of security audit records before policy retention is met. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Cloud REST.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `/services/data/indexes` for `ot_audit`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Set `maxTotalDataSizeMB` with headroom; (2) route verbose protocols to warm/cold tier; (3) expand frozen path; (4) alert if `days_to_full`<90.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0 title=ot_audit
| eval gb=currentDBSizeMB/1024, days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,1)
| eval daily_gb=gb/30
| eval days_to_full=if(daily_gb>0, round((maxTotalDataSizeMB/1024)/daily_gb,1), null())
| table title, gb, days, daily_gb, days_to_full
```

Understanding this SPL

**Audit Index Growth vs Licensed Retention (Capacity Risk) (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.9)** — Forecasts `ot_audit` disk usage to avoid silent loss of security audit records before policy retention is met.

Documented **Data sources**: `/services/data/indexes` for `ot_audit`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Cloud REST. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gb** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **daily_gb** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_to_full** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: daily_gb, days, days_to_full, gb, title.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value, Gauge, Table.
